src/flit/rendering/stack

      • newStackParentData(left = unsetF; top = unsetF; right = unsetF; bottom = unsetF; width = unsetF; height = unsetF): StackParentData

        hitTest
      • hitTest(r: RenderStack; htResult: HitTestResult; position: Offset): bool
        paint
      • paint(r: RenderStack; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset)
        performLayout
      • performLayout(r: RenderStack)

Z-stack rendering. Children paint back-to-front in the order they appear in children. Each child can be either:

  • positioned: absolute placement via StackParentData (left, top, right, bottom, width, height; NaN means "not specified"),
  • non-positioned: laid out under stack constraints and aligned by RenderStack.alignment.

Backs the Stack widget; Positioned contributes the parent data.

Imports

../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/geometry, ../foundation/color

Types

RenderStack = ref object of RenderObject
  alignment*: Alignment
  fit*: StackFit
  children*: seq[RenderStackChild]
Render object behind Stack. Lays out positioned children at absolute offsets and non-positioned children with the stack's alignment. 
RenderStackChild = ref object
  obj*: RenderObject
  pd*: StackParentData
A single child slot in a RenderStack. 
StackFit = enum
  sfLoose, sfExpand, sfPassthrough
Constraint policy for non-positioned stack children.
  • sfLoose: loose constraints (each child picks its size).
  • sfExpand: tight max-size constraints (each child fills).
  • sfPassthrough: pass the stack's own constraints unchanged.
StackParentData = ref object
  left*, top*, right*, bottom*, width*, height*: float32
  positioned*: bool
  offset*: Offset
Parent-data attached to each stack child. NaN-valued fields mean "not specified". positioned is true when at least one position/size field is specified.

Consts

unsetF: float32 = 0x7FF7FFFFFFFFFFFF'f64
Sentinel "not specified" value for StackParentData fields. Pass to Positioned's side parameters to leave them unset.

Procs

proc newStackParentData(left = unsetF; top = unsetF; right = unsetF;
                        bottom = unsetF; width = unsetF; height = unsetF): StackParentData {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds a StackParentData. Any specified field flips positioned to true so the layout treats this child as absolutely positioned.

Methods

method hitTest(r: RenderStack; htResult: HitTestResult; position: Offset): bool {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Walks children in reverse paint order (top-first) so the visually topmost child catches the pointer. Returns on the first child whose bounds contain the point. 
method paint(r: RenderStack; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Paints children in declaration order: index 0 is the back layer, last index is the top layer. Each child is offset by its per-child pd.offset computed during layout. 
method performLayout(r: RenderStack) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                                       forbids: [].}

Two-pass layout for a RenderStack.

Pass 1 (non-positioned children): each receives constraints determined by r.fit (loose / tight-expand / passthrough). The stack's own size is the largest size across these children.

Pass 2 (positioned children): each child's size and position are computed from its StackParentData (left/top/right/bottom/ width/height). When both opposing edges are specified, the extent on that axis is parent_extent - left - right.
